patch.value: Adopt Bun 1.3.11, Node >=22.13, Commander 15, ESLint 10, Vitest 4, and TypeScript 6.0.3. Do not adopt TypeScript 7 until TypeScript-ESLint expands its peer range beyond <6.1.0; retain @types/node 22 to model the supported Node line rather than unrelated Node 26 APIs.
Adopt current Bun and Node 22 toolchain with constrained TypeScript
Choose dependency majors that are current and mutually compatible, with explicit runtime and package-manager ownership.
Declared engines satisfy all direct dependencies; type/lint/test/build pass; constraints and revisit trigger are documented
Registry engine and peer metadata have been queried for every major upgrade.
Predictable maintenance without unsupported version combinations
Avoids false latest-at-any-cost upgrades while closing known vulnerabilities
Current supported toolchain with explicit constraints
The security fix crosses multiple major-version boundaries

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Commander 15 requires Node >=22.12; ESLint 10.7 requires >=22.13 on the Node 22 line.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: TypeScript 7.0.2 is intentionally deferred because @typescript-eslint 8.64.0 declares TypeScript <6.1.0.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: @types/node should match the supported runtime line rather than always follow the registry latest major.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Post-merge Dependabot PR #49 proposed @types/node 26.1.1. It passed mechanically but was closed because the declared runtime remains Node 22; >=23 type-major updates are ignored to preserve runtime/type alignment.

author: codex-root

patch.value: Adopted Node >=22.13, Bun 1.3.11, TypeScript 6.0.3, ESLint 10, Commander 15, matching Node 22 declarations, and explicit automation constraints for incompatible future majors.
Declared engines and peer ranges are mutually compatible and all verification paths pass.
Bun and isolated npm paths pass typecheck, lint, build, 542 tests, audits, and package smokes; PRs #48/#49 supplied live evidence for the intentional constraints.
Compatible current toolchain adopted and enforced

message: Close toolchain decision with merged evidence
